result.reason: Workflow orchestrator with data transformation logic and agent coordination; medium risk since failures are visible but non-destructive

suggestions.description: Test Phase 3 grouping logic: verify that analyzed results are correctly grouped by priority into critical/high/medium/low/skip buckets
Test fallback handling when pipeline returns null/undefined for a file (the typeof result !== 'object' branch) - should produce a default 'analysis failed' entry
Test summary statistics computation: needs_unit count, needs_integration count, has_existing count, total_test_suggestions accumulation across multiple analyzed files
Test default args resolution: dir defaults to '.', extensions defaults to 'ts,js,tsx,jsx,py,go,rs', exclude defaults to correct list when args is undefined or partial
Test early return when discovery agent returns empty array or non-array value - should return {error: 'No source files found'}

Test full workflow execution with mocked agent/pipeline: verify discover prompt includes correct dir/extensions/exclude, pipeline receives discovered files, and final output structure matches expected schema
Test that the agent prompt for file analysis includes the correct file path and all analysis criteria (unit/integration indicators, priority levels)
Test pipeline stage function: given a file path, verify it calls agent with FILE_ANALYSIS_SCHEMA and correct label format 'analyze:{file}'

risk_factors: Summary statistics could be wrong if filter predicates don't match schema (e.g. checking r.needs_unit when result has different key)
Null/undefined pipeline results could cause runtime errors if fallback logic regresses
grouped[priority] could be undefined if priorities array gets out of sync with actual values returned by analysis
Non-array discovery result would silently return error instead of crashing, but the Array.isArray check could mask issues

result.reason: Main plugin orchestrator with complex branching (4 action types), state management, and error handling — dependencies are tested but the orchestration logic itself is not

suggestions.description: Test formatRun() produces correct output with steps including duration calculation, phase labels, and result formatting
Test formatRun() handles edge cases: steps with no completedAt (no duration shown), steps with phase, empty steps array, run with no result
Test execute action='list' returns workflow names with source info, recent runs, and the script template when workflows exist
Test execute action='list' shows helpful empty state message when no workflows are found
Test execute action='status' returns formatted run details for a valid run_id, and error messages for missing run_id or nonexistent run
Test execute action='cancel' sets run status to 'cancelled', updates pending/running steps to 'cancelled', and persists via saveRun
Test execute action='start' with invalid JSON args returns parse error message without creating a run
Test execute action='start' with nonexistent workflow returns not-found message with available workflows hint and template
Test execute action='start' with a workflow that has no default export completes immediately with informative message
Test execute action='start' successfully runs a workflow, persists run with 'completed' status, and returns the result summary
Test execute action='start' catches workflow execution errors, sets run status to 'failed', and returns error message
Test the 'workflow' command handler with no args triggers UI select and sends user message with selected workflow name
Test the 'workflow' command handler with a workflow name and args sends user message including the args string
Test session_start handler sets UI status with correct workflow count when workflows are discovered
Test that step tracking callback in start action correctly deduplicates steps by name+startedAt and updates existing entries via Object.assign

risk_factors: Cancel action may leave orphan steps in 'running' state if status check is wrong
Start action state transitions (running→completed/failed) could be inconsistent if saveRun fails silently
Invalid JSON args could crash instead of returning graceful error if try/catch is removed
Step deduplication logic (find by name+startedAt) could create duplicate entries if timing changes
Command handler's string split on space for workflow name could break with quoted args

result.reason: Core workflow discovery/loading infrastructure with filesystem traversal, deduplication logic, and multiple error-handling paths

suggestions.description: getProjectWorkflowDir returns correct path by joining cwd with '.pi/workflows'
findGitRoot traverses upward and finds .git directory in parent/ancestor directories
findGitRoot returns null when no .git directory exists in any ancestor (reaches filesystem root)
discoverProjectDirs stops traversal at git root and collects all three PROJECT_DIR_NAMES at each level
discoverProjectDirs handles case where no git root exists (falls back to '/' as boundary)
listDir returns only files with valid extensions (.js, .ts, .mjs, .mts) and strips extensions from names
listDir returns empty array when directory does not exist (silent error handling)
listDir filters out files with non-matching extensions (e.g., .json, .md, .txt)
listWorkflows deduplicates by name with project dirs taking priority over global dirs
listWorkflows returns results sorted alphabetically by name
listWorkflows correctly labels entries as 'project' or 'global' source
loadWorkflow tries all extensions in order (.js, .ts, .mjs, .mts) and returns first successful import
loadWorkflow returns null when module exists but lacks meta.name property
loadWorkflow returns null when no matching workflow file exists in any directory
loadWorkflow searches project directories before global directories (project override behavior)
getAllWorkflowDirs returns project dirs first followed by global dirs in correct order

risk_factors: Workflow discovery silently fails if directory traversal logic has bugs (empty catches hide errors)
Project-over-global priority could break if deduplication order is wrong, causing wrong workflow to load
findGitRoot infinite loop risk if dirname/parent logic has edge case on certain filesystems
loadWorkflow returning null on missing meta.name could mask legitimate module loading errors
Extension filtering could miss workflows or include non-workflow files if EXTENSIONS list is wrong
Path traversal could behave differently on Windows vs Unix due to path separator handling

result.reason: Core workflow runtime with complex agent session logic, JSON parsing, and error handling; existing tests cover pipeline and log but not the agent function

suggestions.description: agent() uses options.label as step name when provided, falls back to prompt.slice(0, 60) when label is absent
agent() appends schema instruction to prompt when options.schema is provided, omits it when schema is undefined
agent() strips markdown JSON fences (```json ... ```) from response before parsing when schema is provided
agent() returns raw responseText as string when JSON.parse fails on schema response (graceful fallback)
agent() calls onStep twice: first with status 'running' and startedAt, then with status 'completed', completedAt, and result
agent() sets step status to 'failed' with error message and returns undefined when createAgentSession throws
agent() passes options.phase through to the WorkflowStep
pipeline with empty items array returns empty results array
pipeline with no stages returns the original items unchanged
pipeline with async stage functions resolves correctly
agent() creates a real agent session, sends a prompt, subscribes to message_end events, and extracts assistant text response
agent() calls session.dispose() after prompt completes to clean up resources

risk_factors: JSON parsing logic with fence stripping could silently fail on edge cases (e.g., nested fences, partial fences)
agent() error path could swallow important errors since it catches all and returns undefined
Step status transitions could become inconsistent if onStep is called out of order
Schema instruction injection could corrupt prompts if schema contains special characters
Memory leak risk if session.dispose() is not called on error paths (currently it isn't)

result.reason: File system persistence layer with error handling paths and sorting logic; moderate risk of silent data loss bugs

suggestions.description: saveRun creates the .runs directory recursively and writes a valid JSON file that can be read back
loadRun returns null when the run file does not exist (non-existent runId)
loadRun returns null when the file contains invalid/corrupt JSON
loadRun correctly deserializes a previously saved WorkflowRun with all fields intact
listRuns returns an empty array when the .runs directory does not exist
listRuns filters out non-.json files in the runs directory (e.g. .DS_Store, .tmp files)
listRuns sorts results by updatedAt descending (most recently updated first)
listRuns skips corrupt JSON files gracefully (returns only valid runs, no throw)
saveRun overwrites an existing run file when called with the same runId (update scenario)

risk_factors: Silent data loss if saveRun fails without error propagation
Corrupt JSON files could crash listRuns if error handling regresses
Sort order regression could show stale runs at the top of the list
Non-JSON files in the directory could cause unexpected parse errors
